prompt: 17th-century Rajput miniature painting, opaque watercolor on paper style, depicting a dense royal durbar court scene. In the center, a Maharaja sits in an arched pavilion, his gaze intensely locked onto a single messenger standing among a large crowd of courtiers. Every other figure in the scene has their face turned in strict profile, with large stylized eyes explicitly directed toward either the Maharaja or the messenger, establishing a clear, heavily weighted network of visual focus. Rendered with flat aerial perspective and stacked tiers of figures to show depth without vanishing points. Brilliant, highly saturated color palette of vermillion, lapis lazuli, bright ochre, and gold detailing. Compartmentalized composition using flat geometric architectural arches. Fine, meticulous brushwork on decorative floral textile patterns. Matte paper texture. No diagrams, no text, no labels, no photographic shading, no cast shadows, no Western 3D perspective, no modern technology, no anachronistic elements.
tradition_approach: Applied flat aerial perspective, stacked figure tiers, strict profile faces, compartmentalized architectural framing, saturated vermillion and lapis palette, and uniform line weight without cast shadows to physically map directed visual focus.
